question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a DNS hijack of the mirror REST endpoint resolved by buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to return a crafted node list containing only a subset of real nodes, causing the hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to route all transactions exclusively to those nodes and effectively starving ≥30% of consensus nodes of transaction load?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a network path between the pinger and the mirror REST API perform a man-in-the-middle attack to replace the nodesEnvelope JSON response, injecting only 1–2 node entries so that hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) builds a map excluding ≥30% of real consensus nodes, causing those nodes to receive zero transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user set the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST environment variable to a URL pointing to an attacker-controlled server that returns a crafted nodesEnvelope with only a minority of real node entries, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a network map that excludes ≥30% of consensus nodes from receiving any pinger transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS certificate validation on the cfg.mirrorRest HTTP client to intercept the /api/v1/network/nodes response and selectively omit serviceEndpoints for ≥30% of nodes, causing those nodes to be absent from the netmap passed to hiero.ClientForNetwork()?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK environment variable set it to 'other' and supply a malicious HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST URL, bypassing the hiero.ClientForName() path and instead using buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() with an attacker-controlled node list that excludes ≥30% of real consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the mirror REST response cause buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to return a network map with only a subset of nodes by returning a nodesEnvelope where ≥30% of nodeEntry records have empty NodeAccountID fields, causing those nodes to be silently skipped via the 'if n.NodeAccountID == \

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who can MITM the mirror REST response inject malformed node_account_id strings for ≥30% of nodeEntry records, causing hiero.AccountIDFromString() to return an error and those nodes to be silently skipped via the 'continue' on parse error, resulting in a netmap that excludes ≥30% of consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST response return nodeEntry records for ≥30% of nodes with all serviceEndpoints having empty DomainName and empty IPAddressV4 fields, causing those entries to be skipped by the 'if host == \

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST response return nodeEntry records for ≥30% of nodes with all serviceEndpoints having Port == 0, causing those entries to be skipped by the 'if host == \

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() only fetches a single page of the nodesEnvelope (ignoring the Links.Next pagination field) to cause ≥30% of consensus nodes to be absent from the netmap by ensuring those nodes only appear on page 2+ of the mirror node API response?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the mirror REST API response exploit the missing pagination follow-through in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() — which never follows Links.Next — to ensure that a crafted first-page response contains only a minority of nodes, causing ≥30% of real consensus nodes to be permanently excluded from the SDK client's network map?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a DNS cache poisoning attack against the hostname in cfg.mirrorRest to redirect fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() HTTP requests to an attacker-controlled server, which returns a nodesEnvelope listing only ≤70% of real nodes, causing the remaining ≥30% to be excluded from the hiero.ClientForNetwork() map?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of response body size limits in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() — which uses json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode() without an io.LimitReader — to cause the pinger process to consume excessive memory, potentially crashing it and preventing it from routing transactions to any consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST response inject serviceEndpoint entries with attacker-controlled IP addresses for ≥30% of nodes, causing net.JoinHostPort() to build addresses pointing to attacker-controlled hosts, so those nodes receive no real consensus traffic from the pinger?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST response inject serviceEndpoint entries with attacker-controlled domain names for ≥30% of nodes, causing the DomainName-preferred host selection ('host = strings.TrimSpace(ep.DomainName)') to resolve to attacker-controlled IPs, effectively redirecting those nodes' traffic away from real consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() prefers DomainName over IPAddressV4 in serviceEndpoints to inject a domain name that resolves to a non-routable IP (e.g., 0.0.0.0 or 127.0.0.1) for ≥30% of nodes, causing the SDK to fail to connect to those nodes and effectively removing them from active transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the exponential backoff in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() — 'backoff := cfg.mirrorNodeClientBaseBackoff * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1))' — by causing the mirror REST endpoint to return HTTP 500 errors repeatedly, exhausting all mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries attempts and causing newClient() to return an error, preventing the pinger from starting and routing transactions to any nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror REST endpoint to return HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) responses, which are treated as retryable by 'retry := resp.StatusCode == http.StatusTooManyRequests || resp.StatusCode >= 500', to exhaust all retry attempts in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() and prevent the pinger from building a valid network map, stopping all transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that newClient() only calls buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() once at startup (not periodically) to perform a timing attack — poisoning the mirror REST response only during the pinger's startup window — causing a permanently stale/incomplete network map that excludes ≥30% of nodes for the entire lifetime of the pinger process?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the network map built by buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() is never refreshed after newClient() returns — even if nodes are added or restored — to cause a persistent ≥30% node exclusion by poisoning the initial mirror REST response, with no recovery mechanism until the pinger is restarted?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of HTTPS enforcement on cfg.mirrorRest (which accepts plain http:// URLs) to perform a plaintext HTTP interception of the /api/v1/network/nodes response and replace it with a crafted nodesEnvelope that omits ≥30% of consensus nodes from the serviceEndpoints, causing those nodes to be excluded from the SDK client's routing table?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of response integrity verification (no HMAC, no signature check) on the nodesEnvelope returned by fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to inject a tampered node list that maps ≥30% of node account IDs to attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints, causing those nodes to receive no legitimate consensus transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() uses a single HTTP client with a fixed timeout (cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout) and no connection reuse security to perform a slow-read attack — sending the HTTP response headers immediately but draining the body slowly — causing the context to expire and the pinger to fail to build any network map?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST response return duplicate network map entries (same addr string for multiple nodeAccountIds) in the nodesEnvelope, causing the 'network[addr] = nodeAccountId' map assignment to silently overwrite earlier entries, resulting in some nodes being unreachable and ≥30% of consensus nodes being effectively excluded?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST response inject serviceEndpoint entries with the same IP:port address for multiple different nodeAccountIds, causing the network map to collapse multiple nodes into a single entry via 'network[addr] = nodeAccountId' overwrite, effectively removing ≥30% of distinct nodes from the SDK routing table?
